commit.message:
Implement Provider Qualification and FPO Eligibility — DSD Section 23 CI-117545/CI-67555

Provider Qualification (CI-117545 / CI-67804):
- ProviderTrainingEntity: tracks orientation and annual refresher training completions
  with fiscal-year-aware required hours (8h 2018-20, 16h 2020-22, 24h 2022+)
- ProviderTrainingRepository: queries by provider, type, fiscal year
- ProviderManagementService: getProviderTraining(), recordTraining() (auto-computes
  fiscal year and required hours), getQualificationSummary() (full DSD checklist:
  SOC 426, orientation, background check, CORI tier status, annual training)
- ProviderManagementController: GET/POST /api/providers/{id}/training,
  GET /api/providers/{id}/qualification-summary

FPO Eligibility (CI-67555):
- FPOEligibilityEntity: case-level FPO (Fiscal Personal Option / Public Authority)
  eligibility with full audit history — inactivates old record on update
- FPOEligibilityRepository: active record lookup + full history queries
- CaseManagementService: getFpoEligibility(), getFpoEligibilityHistory(),
  setFpoEligibility() (transactional create-or-replace with history preservation)
- CaseManagementController: GET/PUT /api/cases/{caseId}/fpo-eligibility,
  GET /api/cases/{caseId}/fpo-eligibility/history
- casesApi.js: getFpoEligibility(), setFpoEligibility(), getFpoEligibilityHistory()
- CaseViewFPOEligibilityPage.js: rewritten from UIM stub to full React page using
  wq-* CSS pattern — current status card, edit modal, toggle-able history table,
  status badge (Eligible/Not Eligible), breadcrumb integration
- App.js: added /cases/:caseId/fpo-eligibility route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add SSRF protection, rate limiting, README, and .env.example

Security:
- Block private/internal IP ranges (127.x, 10.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x, etc.)
- Restrict to HTTP/HTTPS protocols only
- Rate limit: 10 requests per IP per hour (in-memory)

Docs:
- Replace default Next.js README with comprehensive project docs
- Add .env.example for easy setup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: pass CreatureTopologyCache to weight_coherence detection functions (#780)

* perf: pass CreatureTopologyCache to weight_coherence detection functions (#770)

Add Option<&CreatureTopologyCache> parameter to detect_incoherent_weight_ratios,
detect_near_constant_paths, and detect_symmetric_cancellation. When provided,
the functions use the shared cache instead of rebuilding local HashMap/HashSet
structures. Falls back to local construction when None is passed.

Updated synapse_specs.rs to pass the shared topology cache.

Benchmarks show ~4x improvement (e.g. 74µs → 17.5µs for 100 neurons).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.0.361: WAN Management improvements, batch QuotaSync, auto-invoicing, customer portal invoices

WAN Management Check:
- RADIUS enforces 1k/1k for all new/unchecked subscribers until port check passes
- BandwidthRuleService skips WAN-blocked users (prevents speed override)
- 1-minute cooldown between re-checks for failed users (was checking every 30s)
- Temp speed restore before re-checking failed users (so /tool/fetch works)
- port_open field synced with wan_check_status (green icon shows only when port verified open)
- Removed grandfather logic — all users must pass port check

QuotaSync Performance:
- Batch fetch ALL active PPP sessions from MikroTik in single API call (GetActiveSessionMap)
- O(1) lookup per subscriber instead of individual API call per user
- Fixes slow sync (was 440+ API calls per cycle, now 1 bulk call)
- Fixes incorrect online count after restart (80 shown instead of 240+)

PortScanService:
- Scans every 2 minutes (was 5), 2s timeout (was 3s), 30s initial delay (was 60s)
- Only sets port_open=true for users with port actually open

MikroTik Client:
- Domain suffix matching for queue names (pppoe-user@domain.com)
- GetActiveSessionMap() for bulk session fetch

Auto Invoice Generation:
- New InvoiceGenerationService runs daily at configured notification time
- Auto-generates invoices for subscribers with auto_invoice=true
- Configurable days before expiry (invoice_days_before_expiry setting)
- Invoice notification templates (email, SMS, WhatsApp)

Invoices Page:
- Printable invoice detail modal with professional layout
- Reseller filtering (resellers only see their own invoices)
- Manual relation loading (fixes GORM gorm:"-" Preload issues)
- Pay invoice with disconnect + reconnect for service activation

Customer Portal:
- Invoices tab — customers can view and print their invoices
- Invoice detail modal with same professional layout

Models:
- Subscriber: added port_open, auto_invoice, deleted_by_id/deleted_by_name fields
- Invoice: added billing_period_start/end, auto_generated fields
- DeletedAt now included in JSON response (for archived subscribers)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: always match notes by folder_name when entering a folder

The UUID-based filter used !n.folder_id as a guard for the name
fallback — if a note had any folder_id set (even a mismatched one)
the folder_name check was skipped, making the folder appear empty.

Now folder_name is always checked as a fallback alongside the UUID
match, so notes reliably show regardless of folder_id state.

commit.message:
fix: resolve all 15 audit findings in web PWA

Auth & state:
- Remove redundant `token` state — use `pass` as single auth guard
- Wrap `loadNotes` in `useCallback` (was redefined on every render)
- Delete dead `auth.js` Netlify function (session tokens never validated)

Data integrity:
- Persist favorites to localStorage (aura-favorites-v1) — survive refresh
  and pull-to-refresh; merged back on every loadNotes call

Dependencies:
- Remove `expo` from web package.json (belongs only in mobile/)
- Remove unused `next-themes` package
- Regenerate package-lock.json

Performance:
- `highlightText` now accepts pre-memoized `RegExp | null` — one RegExp
  instance per query instead of one per rendered note row
- Pre-cache note timestamps as numbers in `noteTimestamps` useMemo —
  eliminates Date construction inside sort comparators
- `useScrollProgress(mobileReaderRef, isReaderOpen)` — mobile reader
  scroll listener only active when reader is open
- Remove deprecated `useRelativeTime` hook, add `enabled` param to
  `useScrollProgress`; move `fmtRelative` to lib/utils.ts

Bug fixes:
- `inkSpills` effect: `clearTimeout` on unmount to prevent setState leak
- Sort menu outside-click: replace `setTimeout` hack with `justOpenedSortRef`
  flag — correct and race-condition free
- `build-index.mjs`: replace hardcoded personal file path validation with
  generic empty-index check
- `netlify/functions/notes.js`: module-level JSON cache (warm Lambda reuse)
- `app/api/notes/route.ts`: `readFileSync` → async `readFile` (non-blocking)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up project: remove obsolete files, fix bugs, add extensible dataset registry

- Add benchmarks.json registry: add new datasets by editing one file, no code changes needed
- Rewrite swebench_loader.py to load dataset config from benchmarks.json dynamically
- Add pre_clone_repos.sh, run_parallel_tmux.sh, resume_batches.sh, setup_batch_repos.sh
- Fix merge_results.py: was looking for wrong output path pattern
- Fix resume_batches.sh: add 'pro' dataset support (was missing)
- Update run_swebench_analysis_v3.py: dataset choices now loaded from registry dynamically
- Rewrite README.md: complete workflow, metrics table, project structure, resource guide
- Update .gitignore: add batch_repos/, logs/, outputs_*/, stop_all_batches.sh

M16 — fix mycelium: fusion strength update + abbreviation filter

Two bugs found during live test:

  connections grew (codebook|muninn had count=101 but strength=5)
- Learned abbreviations were nonsensical (compression->lines) because
  any co-occurring pair was treated as abbreviatable. Now only
  prefix-based pairs qualify (scanner->scan, compression->comp)

commit.message:
refactor(agora): migrate data loading from on-chain queryFilter to Firebase

The indexer (backchain-indexer-service) already writes all Agora events
to Firebase (agora_posts, agora_users, agora_follows, agora_blocks,
agora_stats). Frontend was ignoring this and doing direct on-chain
queryFilter which hit RPC block range limits.

Now: posts, profiles, social graph, blocked authors, and stats all
read from Firebase. Only hasLiked() and getUserProfile() remain
on-chain for real-time accuracy. Eliminates RPC dependency for reads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): add evaluation engine for SOP-based session scoring (#130)

* feat(api): add evaluation engine for SOP-based session scoring

Implements the core eval engine as specified in the PRD and ADR-007:

- DB schema: eval_configs, eval_runs, eval_run_scores tables with RLS
- Eval configs CRUD: create, list, get, update, delete with referential integrity
- Compilation layer: resolves SOP steps + eval configs into executable eval plans
- Evaluators: tool_called, no_tool_called, tool_input_contains, llm_judge
- Assertion engine: equals, contains, gt, lt, exists, matches operators
- Runner: executes eval plan against session messages with short-circuit on required step failure
- Coverage warnings when SOP steps lack eval configs
- Console reporter with structured logging
- RBAC: admin creates/updates/deletes configs; admin+support trigger evals; all roles read

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(api): comprehensive eval engine test suite with security coverage

~190 tests across 9 files covering the full eval pipeline:

Unit tests:
- Evaluator registry, tool_called, no_tool_called, tool_input_contains edge cases
- Assertion engine: all 6 operators with boundary conditions
- LLM judge: mocked AI responses, error handling, timeout behavior
- Compilation layer: extractConnectorToolIds pure function

Integration tests:
- Eval configs CRUD: create all 4 types, validation, RBAC, RLS isolation
- Eval runs: trigger, short-circuit, duplicate detection, coverage warnings
- E2E pipeline: full session → eval → score verification for all evaluator types
- Security: cross-org isolation, unauthenticated access, immutability, invalid input
- Edge cases: deleted configs, soft-deleted tools, zero-config SOPs, duplicate refs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: replace PRD with comprehensive ADR-007, move next steps to GitHub Issues

Remove docs/prd-evaluation-engine.md (not suited for codebase) and narrow
ADR-007 from security-only to the full eval engine decision record covering
architecture, storage model, evaluator design, and security.

Phase 2 items extracted into GitHub Issues:
- #131 transcript windowing
- #132 additional evaluator types
- #133 eval config enhancements
- #134 LLM judge calibration
- #135 OTel tracing pipeline
- #136 Langfuse reporter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(evals): enforce eval-config integrity and shorten DB transactions

* fix(eval-configs): include referencing SOP names in deletion error

Log and surface which SOPs reference an eval config when deletion is
blocked, so users know exactly what to de-assign first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(evals): address PR review feedback

- Extract executeEvaluators(), persistResults(), dispatchReporter()
  from 270-line runEvaluation
- Add per-step try/catch so evaluator throws produce error scores
  instead of marking entire run failed
- Bad regex in assertions now returns result:"error" instead of
  result:"fail" (distinguishable from real compliance failures)
- Add config JSONB validation on eval-config update path (reuse
  create schema's superRefine)
- Hoist CONFIG_SCHEMA_MAP to module level, share connectorToolConfig
  schema between tool_called and no_tool_called
- Import EvaluatorType instead of inline union literals
- Simplify updateEvalConfig with .set(data) directly
- Move getLogger() to module level in console reporter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(evals): use options objects, fix evalConfigId coercion

- Replace 10 positional params in dispatchReporter with options object
- Replace 7 positional params in persistResults with options object
- Remove startTime leak from executeEvaluators signature
- Fix evalConfigId ?? "" → evalConfigId (NOT NULL column, no coercion)
- validateEvalConfig now rejects unknown evaluator types instead of
  silently returning []

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(evals): resolve remaining review feedback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix positional arg assignment: remove == nil check for match results

!str.match(regex) was generating FindStringSubmatch(...) == nil which
always evaluated to false since MethodCall returns *JSValue (never Go
nil). Now uses jsvalue.Not() for truthiness check instead, matching JS
semantics where match returns null (falsy) on non-match.

This was the root cause of --name not consuming the next arg as its
value in the yargs-parser — the !/^-/.test(next) check always failed.

feat(file-input): add minimum file size validation (#2476)

* feat(file-input): add minimum file size validation

Add MinFileSize property to FileInput to complement the existing MaxFileSize
property. This allows developers to reject empty or trivially small files
that are likely erroneous.

Changes:
- Add MinFileSize property to FileInputBase
- Add ValidateMinFileSize method to FileInputValidation
- Wire validation into ValidateValue for single and multiple files
- Add MinFileSize fluent extension method to FileInputExtensions
- Add MinFileSize to UploadContext with server-side validation
- Add comprehensive tests for MinFileSize validation

Closes #2344

- Add batch_get_previous_tiers() and get_tier_changes_today() to db/scoring.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reddit MCP collector — dynamic port, relaxed filter, startup health check

Bug #3: Replace hardcoded port 18765 with dynamic free port allocation
via socket bind to port 0. Prevents collision when running concurrently.

Bug #4: Replace exact substring name filter with _post_matches_company()
that also matches domain root (without TLD) and individual name tokens
>=3 chars. Catches abbreviations and product names before Claude classification.

Bug #6: Replace blind 2s sleep with health check loop (up to 10s) that
polls the MCP server endpoint until it responds. Prevents silent failure
on slow machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: search bar debounce — move debounce from x-model to @input

The debounce on x-model delayed updating this.search, but @input fired
loadAccounts() immediately with the stale value. Now the model updates
instantly and the API call is debounced instead.

Replace Gemini crop with OpenCV face detection for banners

Gemini was failing on all images (NSFW safety filter returned empty
content), so all banners used dumb center-crop that cut off faces.

window around them, with center-crop fallback when no faces found.

feat: Sebol jako ekspert digital marketingu przed tworzeniem kampanii

Przed stworzeniem każdej kampanii, Sebol teraz:
1. Zbiera brakujące wymagane pola (klient, budżet, link)
2. Analizuje kampanię jak ekspert performance marketingu
3. Proaktywnie pyta o rzeczy które mogą znacząco wpłynąć na wyniki
   (retargeting, faza uczenia, format kreacji, broad vs targetowanie etc.)
4. Czeka na potwierdzenie ("zaczynaj") lub przyjmuje zmiany
5. Dopiero po potwierdzeniu buduje kampanię w Meta Ads

State machine: collecting → expert_review → build
Słowa kluczowe potwierdzenia: "zaczynaj", "dawaj", "buduj", "go" etc.
Zmiany: user pisze co zmienić → Sebol re-analizuje

Nowa funkcja: generate_campaign_expert_analysis() w campaign_creator.py
- Używa Claude claude-sonnet-4-20250514 jako eksperta Meta Ads
- Analizuje cel, budżet, targeting, platformy, kreacje, link
- Max 220 słów, bezpośrednio, po polsku

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pass UDP socket info via ext_info to QUIC listeners for complete getInfo()

QUIC listeners were missing address/family details in getInfo() because
the HttpListener was created with no_bind=True (no TCP socket to query).
Pass the UDP socket info (address, address_desc, family, familystr, port,
hostname, hostname_desc) via ext_info so getListeners()/getListenerInfo()
return complete information for QUIC listeners.

commit.message:
fix: actualizar imagen base Docker a node:22-alpine (v1.5.5)

node:sqlite (usado en backup/route.ts para validar schema SQLite)
requiere Node.js >= 22.5. La imagen node:20-alpine causaba error
de tipos en el build de producción.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): suppress NestJS 11 LegacyRouteConverter path-to-regexp warnings

NestJS 11 uses path-to-regexp v8 which replaces `*` wildcards with
`{*path}` named parameters. NestJS internally generates `/api/*` when
setGlobalPrefix("api") is used, triggering LegacyRouteConverter warnings.

Known NestJS core issue (nestjs/nest#14530) — routes work correctly
via auto-conversion, warnings are cosmetic.

Fix applied in two layers:
- Bootstrap logger: filters during NestFactory.create()
- AppLoggerService.warn(): filters at runtime

Also suppresses verbose route-mapping logs that hit Railway log rate limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: full codebase audit + roadmap v10 with audit-derived backlog (RN-073)

6-domain forensic audit (code quality, test integrity, downstream compatibility,
accessibility, infrastructure/security, documentation) producing 166 findings.
Archive 8 completed items (RN-058, RN-071, RN-017, RN-059, RN-068, RN-069,
RN-070, RN-073) and create 7 new roadmap items (RN-074–RN-080) from audit
findings, prioritised by downstream adoption impact.

Revert Grafana to NodePort, update inventory IP to WSL2 NAT network

WSL2 switched from mirrored to NAT mode — host IP changed to 192.168.196.67.
Reverted Grafana from hostNetwork back to NodePort:38888 (no longer needed
since WSL2 NAT mode has separate IPs for Windows and WSL2).
